scope_boundary: Public-safe local external Circle sparse-attention receipt import only. The slice records one pinned `CC-AI-CONTRACT-SPARSE-001` structural fixture at Circle commit `63b0f511`, `coverage_complete=false`, first missed lag `5`, uncovered intervals, complete repair window `119`, two required theorem IDs, two planner recommendations, contract fingerprint `c23809cef9b821b1e4f9cabf53fcac724a0757bf3f86594e1d12710fe0cd9ec1`, command-output digests, and a passing Circle CLI test summary. It does not prove sparse-attention coverage success, deployed sparse-attention behavior, retrieval quality, long-context quality, model quality, speed, memory savings, transfer, safety, ASI, or Coil chapter-core promotion.
transition_reason: The sparse-attention receipt slice makes one Circle sparse coverage/gap structural fixture concrete in the book and closes the stale 'no sparse-attention receipt import' blocker for the Coil chapter. The evidence remains an external-project receipt import with an explicit `coverage_complete=false` boundary, no deployed ASI-side sparse-attention trace, no retrieval or long-context workload, no runtime or memory measurement, no model-quality result, and no independent review, so it blocks promotion rather than moving support state.
